game_name: Space Quest IV: Roger Wilco and the Time Rippers

danger_signals.visual_form: Robotic enemy characters with distinctive mechanical appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; Sludge Vohaul's enforcer presence indicates danger zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Ruins of Xenon's main city; destroyed infrastructure and environmental decay
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger: catastrophic disaster has occurred; unsafe zone ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual distortion effect indicating temporal displacement occurring
danger_signals.meaning: Disorientation signal; player is entering new, unknown territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Fortified AI supercomputer installation with hostile architecture

safety_signals.visual_form: Allied military personnel with distinct uniform/equipment differentiation from androids
Civilian commercial zone with peaceful architecture contrasting to ruined cities
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven zone; non-hostile civilian space

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible objects appearing in inventory with positive feedback
safety_signals.meaning: Progression safety; player is gaining resources to counter threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Androids as visual markers of Vohaul's command
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Androids with mechanical, non-human appearance; hostile behavior differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Vohaul's base structure; digitized AI presence infecting Xenon's systems

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military uniform and equipment distinct from android enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Time-rift opening; presence of time-travel ship technology

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Civilian characters; bar patrons retelling Roger's heroics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bar setting; commercial spaces (Estros shopping mall); homeworld infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Functional bar environment; Xenon homeworld accessible; Aluminum Mallard spacecraft intact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs in bar listening to Roger's stories; normal social interaction possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ruined cityscape; destroyed infrastructure; AI supercomputer installation visible; environmental decay evident
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No functional population; automated threats (androids) patrol ruins; hostile world-state manifest through danger presence

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Estros planet timeline (Space Quest X parody)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shopping mall with civilian retail environment; peaceful architectural style; girlfriend character present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs engaging in normal commerce; shopping trip activities; social interaction possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: AI supercomputer base with virus-infected systems; androids multiplying; civilization collapse visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Androids pursuing Roger across timelines; systematic threat escalation; world becoming uninhabitable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Supercomputer reformatting; androids ceasing hostile action; Xenon beginning recovery
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Son character reveals future knowledge; farewell interaction possible; temporal stability returning

environmental_storytelling.example: Exploration of devastated Xenon city reveals recording explaining AI infection; players discover cause of catastrophe through environmental investigation
Different planetary environments (Xenon present vs. future; Estros) visually communicate temporal displacement and narrative structure
Bar patrons listening to Roger's heroics establish his reputation; future ex-girlfriend on Estros indicates romantic plot thread; son character reveal establishes emotional stakes
Vohaul's fortified base appearance contrasts with civilian spaces; visual design communicates hostile vs. safe zones
Time-travel ship acquisition and use visualizes escalating player capability; stealing androids' ship marks power inversion moment

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Tracking Roger across timelines; aggressive mechanical movement patterns; relentless pursuit
Forced through time-rift by soldier; fleeing from androids; infiltrating enemy base
Opening time-rift for escape; instructing Roger; later revealed as future son
In trouble on Estros; joins shopping trip; provides navigation assistance
Digitizing consciousness into supercomputer; infecting Xenon systems; hunting Roger across time
Population killed or enslaved by virus; infrastructure destroyed; no independent action possible

notes: Space Quest IV uses time-travel narrative framework to visually communicate multiple world-states and dangers. The contrast between peaceful present-day spaces (bar, mall) and devastated future Xenon serves as primary danger communication method. Faction identity relies on mechanical vs. organic visual distinction (androids vs. humans). Environmental ruins tell story of catastrophic threat. The game's parody structure (referencing fictional Space Quest X and XII) itself becomes meta-commentary on how sequels escalate stakes and danger. Point-and-click interface allows players to examine world details that reinforce storytelling about faction conflict and temporal stakes.
sources_used: wikipedia: Space Quest IV - Roger Wilco and the Time Rippers
